issue.author: yuxuanchen1997

comments.author: yuxuanchen1997
comments.body:
I am not particularly convinced that `MemoryDepChecker` is checking that obj in the loop statement `MyObject* obj = arr[j];` can alias one another in adjacent iterations. I don't see runtime checks being generated for this array of pointers either. (However, they are generated for `arr[j]` and `arr[pos]`) Furthormore, the logic in `AccessAnalysis::canCheckPtrAtRT` ignores alias sets with a single pointer write and no reads. I don't quite get why but that directly caused the unsafety to be ignored I think.

@fhahn, I saw that you worked on runtime checks and wondering if I can get some pointers here. Should runtime checks be generated for this array of pointers or should LAA just conclude that it's unsafe to vectorize? I don't quite get the intricacies in the pass yet. I would appreciate some explanations on how to look at this issue.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Thanks, let me take a look

comments.author: yuxuanchen1997
comments.body:
@fhahn 

I thought that this might be helpful: because that I have a small repro it's not too difficult to run a local bisect. Bisect pointed to 20d798bd47ec5191de1b2a8a031da06a04e612e1.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Ok this looks unrelated to #69909 and 20d798bd47ec5191de1b2a8a031da06a04e612e1 which only exposes the issue for that particular case. Preparing a fix.

issue.title: LoopVectorize Miscompilation with Aliases in clang 15+

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @test_indirect_read_write_loop_also_modifies_pointer_array(ptr noundef %arr) {
entry:
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.1 ], [ 8, %entry ]
  %arr.addr.0.i = phi ptr [ %incdec.ptr.i, %loop.1 ], [ %arr, %entry ]
  %incdec.ptr.i = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, i64 1
  %0 = load ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %tobool.not.i = icmp eq ptr %0, null
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 8
  br i1 %tobool.not.i, label %loop.1.exit, label %loop.1

loop.1.exit:                                      ; preds = %loop.1
  %iv.lcssa = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.1 ]
  br label %loop.2

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1.exit
  %iv.1 = phi i64 [ 0, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop.2 ]
  %iv.2 = phi i64 [ %iv.lcssa, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %gep.iv.1 = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.1
  %l.1 = load ptr, ptr %gep.iv.1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %l.2 = load i64, ptr %l.1, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %inc = add i64 %l.2, 1
  store i64 %inc, ptr %l.1, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %iv.2.next = add nsw i64 %iv.2, 1
  %gep.iv.2 = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.2
  store ptr %l.1, ptr %gep.iv.2, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %iv.1.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv.1, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %iv.1.next, 1000
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.2, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret void
}

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"any pointer", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C/C++ TBAA"}
!4 = !{!5, !6, i64 0}
!5 = !{!"", !6, i64 0}
!6 = !{!"long long", !2, i64 0}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @test_indirect_read_write_loop_does_not_modify_pointer_array(ptr noundef %arr, ptr noalias noundef %arr2) {
entry:
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.1 ], [ 8, %entry ]
  %arr.addr.0.i = phi ptr [ %incdec.ptr.i, %loop.1 ], [ %arr, %entry ]
  %incdec.ptr.i = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, i64 1
  %0 = load ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %tobool.not.i = icmp eq ptr %0, null
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 8
  br i1 %tobool.not.i, label %loop.1.exit, label %loop.1

loop.1.exit:                                      ; preds = %loop.1
  %iv.lcssa = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.1 ]
  br label %loop.2

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1.exit
  %iv.1 = phi i64 [ 0, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop.2 ]
  %iv.2 = phi i64 [ %iv.lcssa, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %gep.iv.1 = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.1
  %l.1 = load ptr, ptr %gep.iv.1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %l.2 = load i64, ptr %l.1, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %inc = add i64 %l.2, 1
  store i64 %inc, ptr %l.1, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %iv.2.next = add nsw i64 %iv.2, 1
  %gep.arr2.iv.1 = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %arr2, i64 %iv.1
  %gep.arr2.iv.2 = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %arr2, i64 %iv.2
  %l.3 = load i64, ptr %gep.arr2.iv.1, align 8
  %inc.2 = add i64 %l.3, 5
  store i64 %inc.2, ptr %gep.arr2.iv.2, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %iv.1.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv.1, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %iv.1.next, 1000
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.2, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret void
}

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"any pointer", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C/C++ TBAA"}
!4 = !{!5, !6, i64 0}
!5 = !{!"", !6, i64 0}
!6 = !{!"long long", !2, i64 0}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @test_indirect_read_loop_also_modifies_pointer_array(ptr noundef %arr) {
entry:
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.1 ], [ 8, %entry ]
  %arr.addr.0.i = phi ptr [ %incdec.ptr.i, %loop.1 ], [ %arr, %entry ]
  %incdec.ptr.i = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, i64 1
  %0 = load ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %tobool.not.i = icmp eq ptr %0, null
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 8
  br i1 %tobool.not.i, label %loop.1.exit, label %loop.1

loop.1.exit:                                      ; preds = %loop.1
  %iv.lcssa = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.1 ]
  br label %loop.2

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1.exit
  %iv.1 = phi i64 [ 0, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop.2 ]
  %iv.2 = phi i64 [ %iv.lcssa, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %gep.iv.1 = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.1
  %l.1 = load ptr, ptr %gep.iv.1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %l.2 = load i64, ptr %l.1, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %inc = add i64 %l.2, 1
  %iv.2.next = add nsw i64 %iv.2, 1
  %gep.iv.2 = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.2
  store i64 %l.2, ptr %gep.iv.2, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %iv.1.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv.1, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %iv.1.next, 1000
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.2, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret void
}

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"any pointer", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C/C++ TBAA"}
!4 = !{!5, !6, i64 0}
!5 = !{!"", !6, i64 0}
!6 = !{!"long long", !2, i64 0}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @test_indirect_write_loop_also_modifies_pointer_array(ptr noundef %arr) {
entry:
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.1 ], [ 8, %entry ]
  %arr.addr.0.i = phi ptr [ %incdec.ptr.i, %loop.1 ], [ %arr, %entry ]
  %incdec.ptr.i = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, i64 1
  %0 = load ptr, ptr %arr.addr.0.i, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %tobool.not.i = icmp eq ptr %0, null
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 8
  br i1 %tobool.not.i, label %loop.1.exit, label %loop.1

loop.1.exit:                                      ; preds = %loop.1

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1.exit

  %iv.2 = phi i64 [ %iv.lcssa, %loop.1.exit ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %gep.iv.1 = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.1
  %l.1 = load ptr, ptr %gep.iv.1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %inc = add i64 %iv.1, 1
  store i64 %inc, ptr %l.1, align 8, !tbaa !4
  %iv.2.next = add nsw i64 %iv.2, 1
  %gep.iv.2 = getelementptr inbounds ptr, ptr %arr, i64 %iv.2
  store ptr %l.1, ptr %gep.iv.2, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %iv.1.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv.1, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %iv.1.next, 1000
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.2, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret void
}

!1 = !{!"any pointer", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C/C++ TBAA"}
